title: 悬崖上的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\241-悬崖上的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Frankie meets with Mr. Spragg to discuss Allen Castles, who had previously sought legal advice regarding John Savage's will. Mr. Spragg explains that he advised Castles against pursuing a lawsuit because Savage's distant relatives in Australia lack the funds to fight it. He suggests that while the case involves potential undue influence by a woman, the legal reality makes a challenge difficult.

Mr. Spragg recounts that last November, during a voyage from the United States to England, Mr. Savage met a beautiful married woman, Mrs. Templeton. Under her influence, Savage began visiting her villa in Chipping Sutton frequently. Convinced he had cancer despite a distinguished expert's testimony to the contrary, a highly anxious Mr. Savage drafted a will through a renowned lawyer. The will left a £700,000 tax-free inheritance to Mrs. Templeton and the remainder to various charities. That same night, Mr. Savage committed suicide by swallowing trichloroacetate, leaving a note stating he wished to avoid a long, painful death.

A jury ruled the death a suicide caused by mental illness. Although Mr. Castles believed the will was out of character—noting that Mr. Savage typically preferred leaving money to blood relatives—the will was authenticated. Mr. Spragg notes that Mrs. Templeton has since left for the south of France and refuses to communicate. After the meeting, Frankie leaves, remarking that Mr. Spragg is "remarkable," while Mr. Spragg is left pondering the identity of the "young duke" mentioned in their conversation. Meanwhile, Bobbie worries about Moira's disappearance, considering the possibility that she is being held captive in a sanatorium.
